item.title: Photality 0.94
item.description:
Version 0.94

  • One-click autofocus. With an Astroasis electronic focuser, Photality maps the focus curve and drives to its peak — and a saved Focus Point brings you back to perfect focus in one click, even mid-capture. An experimental mode can even re-tune focus automatically between partial-phase captures as the air cools toward totality.
  • Your frames survive a dying drive. If the output disk fails, fills, or proves too slow mid-capture, saving switches automatically to a backup folder (your internal disk by default) and keeps going.
  • Pre-flight check. Press Start and get a green-light checklist: camera, cable quality, focus, contact times, disk space and write speed, power, and voice alerts — before the plan arms.
  • Honest plan timing. The editor now models the camera's real per-frame costs (including long-exposure stream restarts), so a totality plan that says it fits actually fits.
  • True-color preview. Color cameras now show a properly debayered, white-balanced live view — no more green cast or wrong brightness — and a new connect-time guard repairs cameras whose white-balance registers were left corrupted by other software.
  • Bad-cable detection. A USB-C cable that silently downgrades your camera to USB 2.0 now gets called out at connect, instead of surfacing as mysterious preview failures.
  • Sharper safety and voice alerts: save failures speak a short, useful script; update checks pause during capture; and a raft of reliability fixes for focuser, preview, and speech edge cases.
  • Captures now save to ~/Pictures/Photality by default, and the website has a new Getting Started guide.
